published_prior_art.cite: Hahn1998Econometrica + HiranoImbensRidder2003Econometrica + ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML + ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022AutoDML: standard ATE EIF/DML/ADML baseline

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orth_uniform_v2 angle1: prior rho=0 residual-distance frontier rejected; this is the requested primitive dictionary/Jacobian/weight pivot

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO + Hansen1982Econometrica + ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022AutoDML: known LRO/GMM/Riesz machinery, no explicit ATE CEINR Gram-alignment classification found
Hansen1982Econometrica + ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO: overidentified GMM aggregation is known; the finite-support ATE span/alignment separation is not located as a named result

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022AutoDML conflates DOI 10.3982/ECTA18515, which is 'Automatic Debiased Machine Learning of Causal and Structural Effects' in Econometrica 90(3):967-1027, with the different 'via Riesz Regression' title.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: the generic CEINR-admissible iff is paired only with algebraic reductions and an underspecified candidate witness, not a concrete non-corner CEINR-generated tuple with cell probabilities, b_J, A_J, and W_J.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship witness cites Hansen1982Econometrica and ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO by bibkey only in the §8 block, with no section/equation/page anchor for the specific overidentified aggregation gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey omits the closer Riesz/AutoDML equivalence comparator Bruns-Smith et al., 'Augmented balancing weights as linear regression', which directly studies AutoDML/Riesz-style augmented weighting equivalences.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The CEINR-admissible class is still defined by reference to being generated 'in the sense of' the LRO paper rather than by explicit primitive moment equations, so the domain of the iff is not pinned.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumption finite-support witness already requires the candidate dictionary to have S_J=0 and T_J>0, so the conjecture assumes its load-bearing separation.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The proof sketch says the main work is verifying the witness is CEINR-generated, but gives no explicit CEINR equations or concrete cell-level construction route.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the CEINR-admissible prose predicate with explicit moment/Jacobian/first-step influence-function equations copied into §6-§7, so Conjecture 1 quantifies over a mathematically checkable class.

Remove S_J=0 and T_J>0 from the finite-support assumption and give a full two-cell witness table: cell probabilities, propensities, residual variances, b_J rows, A_J, W_J, and the resulting S_J,T_J,Delta_J.
Correct the AutoDML bibliography and add location anchors for Hansen's GMM aggregation and CEINR's LRO report formula in the §8 comparison blocks.
Add Bruns-Smith et al. on augmented balancing weights/Riesz equivalence to Related work and explain why its linear-regression equivalence does not subsume the LRO Gram-alignment certificate.

recommended_next_step: Revise locally: pin the CEINR predicate, de-circularize and instantiate the witness, fix the AutoDML bibitem, and add location-level anchors before re-claiming flagship.
